response.overview: features the sky and landscapes of the Canberra region of Australia – in particular the Brindabella Ranges – captured in near-infrared.  This feature-length film (140+ minutes in total) focuses on the interplay of mountain light, air and water as these elements are transformed across the seasons – from clouds to mist, rain and snow – then frost and ice – and onto creeks and rivers. It explores both the wider montane vistas of the Brindabellas and the more intimate details of the natural flows that are created by these mountains and, in turn, shape the very landscapes they arise from.
response.title: Brindabellas: Edge of Light
